title: श्री शिव पञ्चवक्त्र स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
ॐ सद्योजाताय विद्महे सिद्धपुरुषाय धीमहि।
तन्नो ब्रह्मा प्रचोदयात्॥१॥

सद्योजातं प्रपद्यामि सद्योजाताय वै नमो नमः।
भवे भवे नातिभवे भवस्व मां भवोद्भवाय नमः॥२॥

वामदेवाय नमो ज्येष्ठाय नमः श्रेष्ठाय नमो रुद्राय नमः।
कालाय नमः कलविकरणाय नमो बलविकरणाय नमः।
बलाय नमो बलप्रमथनाय नमः सर्वभूतदमनाय नमः।
मनोन्मनाय नमः॥३॥

अघोरेभ्योऽथ घोरेभ्यो घोरघोरतरेभ्यः।
सर्वेभ्यः सर्वशर्वेभ्यो नमस्ते अस्तु रुद्ररूपेभ्यः॥४॥

तत्पुरुषाय विद्महे महादेवाय धीमहि।
तन्नो रुद्रः प्रचोदयात्॥५॥

ईशानः सर्वविद्यानामीश्वरः सर्वभूतानां
ब्रह्माधिपतिर्ब्रह्मणोऽधिपतिर्ब्रह्मा शिवो मे अस्तु सदाशिवोम्॥६॥

पञ्चवक्त्रं दशभुजं शूलखड्गधरं शिवम्।
त्रिनेत्रं पञ्चवर्णाभं नमामि परमेश्वरम्॥७॥

सद्योजातमुखं प्राच्यां वामदेवं तथोत्तरम्।
अघोरं दक्षिणे वक्त्रं तत्पुरुषं तु पश्चिमे॥८॥

ऊर्ध्ववक्त्रमीशानं सर्वोर्ध्वगतिदायकम्।
पञ्चवक्त्रं महादेवं प्रणमामि सनातनम्॥९॥

पञ्चब्रह्मस्वरूपाय पञ्चकृत्यपरायणम्।
सृष्टिस्थितिलयानां च कर्तारं शङ्करं भजे॥१०॥

॥ इति श्री शिव पञ्चवक्त्र स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Om Sadyojataya Vidmahe Siddhapurushaya Dhimahi.
Tanno Brahma Prachodayat.||1||

Sadyojatam Prapadyami Sadyojataya Vai Namo Namah.
Bhave Bhave Natibhave Bhavasva Mam Bhavodbhavaya Namah.||2||

Vamadevaya Namo Jyeshthaya Namah Shreshthaya Namo Rudraya Namah.
Kalaya Namah Kalavikaranaya Namo Balavikaranaya Namah.
Balaya Namo Balapramathanaya Namah Sarvabhutadamanaya Namah.
Manonmanaya Namah.||3||

Aghorebhyo'tha Ghorebhyo Ghoraghoratarebhyah.
Sarvebhyah Sarvasharvebhyo Namaste Astu Rudrarupebhyah.||4||

Tatpurushaya Vidmahe Mahadevaya Dhimahi.
Tanno Rudrah Prachodayat.||5||

Ishanah Sarvavidyanam Ishvarah Sarvabhutanam
Brahmadhipatir Brahmano'dhipatir Brahma Shivo Me Astu Sadashivom.||6||

Panchavaktram Dashabhujam Shulakhadgadharam Shivam.
Trinetram Panchavarṇabham Namami Parameshvaram.||7||

Sadyojatamukham Prachyam Vamadevam Tathottaram.
Aghoram Dakshine Vaktram Tatpurusham Tu Pashchime.||8||

Urdhvavaktram Ishanam Sarvordhvagatidayakam.
Panchavaktram Mahadevam Pranamami Sanatanam.||9||

Panchabrahmasvarupaya Panchakrtyaparayanam.
Srishtisthitilaya̅nam Cha Kartaram Shankaram Bhaje.||10||

|| Iti Shri Shiva Panchavaktra Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ॐ सद्योजात (तत्काल उत्पन्न) रूप को हम जानते हैं, सिद्ध पुरुष का हम ध्यान करते हैं, वह ब्रह्म हमें प्रेरित करें।

सद्योजात रूप की शरण लेता हूँ, सद्योजात को बारम्बार नमस्कार। जन्म-जन्म में अत्यधिक जन्म न हो, संसार से उत्पन्न शिव को नमस्कार।

वामदेव को नमस्कार, ज्येष्ठ को नमस्कार, श्रेष्ठ को नमस्कार, रुद्र को नमस्कार। काल को, काल के विकार को, बल के विकार को, बल को, बल का मर्दन करने वाले को, समस्त भूतों को दमन करने वाले को, मनोन्मन (चित्त से परे) को नमस्कार।

अघोर, घोर और घोर से भी घोरतर सभी रूपों को — सर्वशर्व रुद्र के समस्त रूपों को नमस्कार।

तत्पुरुष को हम जानते हैं, महादेव का ध्यान करते हैं, वह रुद्र हमें प्रेरित करें।

ईशान सर्व विद्याओं के स्वामी हैं, सर्व भूतों के ईश्वर हैं, ब्रह्म के अधिपति हैं — ऐसे शिव सदाशिव मुझे प्राप्त हों।

पाँच मुख वाले, दस भुजा वाले, शूल और खड्ग धारण करने वाले, त्रिनेत्र, पञ्चवर्ण आभा वाले परमेश्वर शिव को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

पूर्व में सद्योजात मुख, उत्तर में वामदेव, दक्षिण में अघोर, पश्चिम में तत्पुरुष।

ऊर्ध्व मुख ईशान है जो सर्वोच्च गति प्रदान करता है। ऐसे पञ्चवक्त्र सनातन महादेव को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ।

पञ्चब्रह्म स्वरूप, पाँच कृत्यों (सृष्टि, स्थिति, लय, तिरोधान, अनुग्रह) में परायण शंकर — सृष्टि, स्थिति और लय के कर्ता को मैं भजता हूँ।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीपञ्चवक्त्रस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य वामदेव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीपञ्चवक्त्रशिव देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। शिवानुग्रहशक्तिः। पञ्चब्रह्मसाक्षात्कारः कीलकम्। श्रीशिवप्रीत्यर्थे पाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Invokes the blessings of all five faces of Lord Shiva
Invokes protection from the five elements and their imbalances
Leads to deep spiritual awakening and self-realization
Traditionally recited for removal of fear, ignorance, and all forms of negativity
Invokes the blessing of knowledge of pancha brahma tattva
Powerful for Maha Shivaratri and Pradosha worship
description: Shiva Panchavaktra Stotram — श्री शिव पञ्चवक्त्र स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Shiva. Attributed to Yajurveda (Taittiriya Aranyaka) and Traditional Shaiva Texts, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow knowledge of Pancha Brahma tattva and invoke the blessings of all five faces of Lord Shiva. It is also said to remove fear, ignorance, and all forms of negativity. This sacred text is ideally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Pradosh for enhanced blessings. With 10 verses and a reading time of about 7 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Shiva Panchavaktra Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Shiva Panchavaktra Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to the five-faced Shiva for protection, knowledge, and spiritual awakening.
source: Yajurveda (Taittiriya Aranyaka) and Traditional Shaiva Texts
relatedSearches: Five faces of Shiva Sadyojata Vamadeva Aghora Tatpurusha Ishana

seoFaqs.question: What are the five faces of Shiva named in the Panchavaktra Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The five faces are Sadyojata, Vamadeva, Aghora, Tatpurusha and Ishana. Each face represents one of Shiva's five cosmic functions, called the panchakritya: Sadyojata governs srishti (creation), Vamadeva governs sthiti (preservation), Aghora governs samhara (dissolution), Tatpurusha governs tirobhava (concealment) and Ishana governs anugraha (revealing grace). Together these five functions encompass the entire cosmological cycle. The stotram salutes each face individually with its associated colour, direction and attribute, then unites them in a final salutation to Sadashiva, the supreme five-faced form. This panchakritya scheme is the doctrinal core of Shaiva Siddhanta and Kashmir Shaiva theology.

seoFaqs.question: Where in the Vedic corpus do the five-face mantras originate?
seoFaqs.answer: The five face-mantras are preserved in the Taittiriya Aranyaka of the Krishna Yajurveda as the Panchabrahma Mantras, often called the Panchabrahma Samhita section. The five mantras are Sadyojatam prapadyami, Vamadevaya namo, Aghorebhyo, Tatpurushaya vidmahe and Ishanah sarva-vidyanam. Each mantra is associated with one of the five faces and one of the five elements (earth, water, fire, air, ether respectively in some schemes). The stotram form circulating in modern devotional collections is a later compilation that takes these Vedic mantras as its doctrinal framework and elaborates each into a stanza of praise. The Vedic origin gives the stotram unusual authority within the Shaiva textual hierarchy.

seoFaqs.question: What are the directional and chromatic associations of each face?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition assigns specific directions and colours to each face. Tatpurusha faces East and is gold or yellow in hue. Aghora faces South and is dark blue or black. Sadyojata faces West and is white. Vamadeva faces North and is saffron or red. Ishana faces upward (urdhva) and is the colour of pure crystal or sphatika. This four-cardinal-plus-upward scheme means that the Pancanana form requires five visible faces, which is rendered in temple sculpture by carving four faces around the lingam shaft and one on top, often called a Mukhalinga. The Panchavaktra Stotram is chanted while circumambulating such a Mukhalinga to invoke each face from its proper direction in sequence.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Panchavaktra Stotram relate to Sadashiva worship?
seoFaqs.answer: Sadashiva is the form of Shiva that is conceived with five faces and ten arms, representing the totality of the panchakritya. The Panchavaktra Stotram is essentially a hymn to Sadashiva, since invoking all five faces simultaneously is invoking the integrated Sadashiva. Shaiva Agama and Shaiva Siddhanta texts treat Sadashiva as the highest ontological category among manifest Shiva-tattvas, immediately below the unmanifest Para-Shiva. The Mukhalinga form widely seen at Pashupatinath in Kathmandu, at Tala in Chhattisgarh and at numerous Cambodian and Indonesian temples, is the iconographic counterpart of this stotram. Reciting the hymn before such a Mukhalinga unites verbal and visual worship of the Sadashiva concept.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Panchavaktra Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is recited daily as part of nitya-puja in Shaiva Siddhanta and Smarta households, on Mondays and Pradosham evenings, during Shravana and Karthika months, and on Maha Shivaratri. Agamic temple priests chant the Panchabrahma Mantras (and the derived Panchavaktra Stotram) at the start of every major Shiva-abhishekam to invoke all five aspects of Shiva before the elemental ablutions begin. Sadhakas working with the Panchakshara mantra often pair it with a Panchavaktra recitation, since each of the five syllables of Namah Shivaya can be aligned with one of the five faces. The recommended posture is seated facing east before a Shiva lingam or Mukhalinga, after a bath, with vibhuti applied.

seoFaqs.question: What contemplative practice accompanies the recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Advanced practitioners pair each verse of the Panchavaktra Stotram with a deliberate visualisation of the corresponding face in its assigned colour and direction, while contemplating the cosmic function it represents. The standard sequence visualises Sadyojata in the West and contemplates the universe coming into being, then Vamadeva in the North contemplating preservation, then Aghora in the South contemplating dissolution of accumulated karma, then Tatpurusha in the East contemplating the concealment that allows individuality, and finally Ishana above contemplating the revealing grace that grants liberation. This pancha-darshana meditation transforms the hymn from a devotional recitation into a structured Shaiva contemplative practice, and is taught in Shaiva Siddhanta lineages as preparation for higher Sadashiva worship.
